title: UML Diagram Visual Quality
summary: The UML Class Diagram is visually flawed and its structure cannot easily be understood.

raw.model_snippet: The UML Class Diagram is visually flawed and its structure cannot easily be understood.
raw.classifier_raw_response:
BADUML

Reasons:
- Several relationships are unclear or non-standard in notation, especially the mixed use of arrows/diamonds without visible multiplicities or labels.
- Some classes omit full type information for attributes like `dom`, `blockNode`, and `expressionNode`, reducing UML completeness.
- Visibility notation is inconsistent in meaning/style for what appear to be helper/internal methods.
- The diagram is readable overall, but it lacks important UML details such as multiplicities, role names, and clearer distinction between association, aggregation, and dependency.
- Dashed dependencies at the bottom are not very clearly presented relative to the rest of the model.

So while it is fairly legible and class boxes are structured, it does not fully meet strong UML compliance expectations.

judge.rationale: Picture #/pictures/1 is clearly a UML class diagram showing classes with attributes/methods and relationships, so per the override the finding must be approved.
